news.headline: uniQure CEO sells shares after option exercise under 10b5-1 plan
news.summary: CEO Matthew Kapusta exercised options on 21,082 ordinary shares at $19.39 and sold the shares in three open-market trades between roughly $44 and $46, under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan. The sale followed the sharp run-up in uniQure shares after the June FDA news.

news.headline: H.C. Wainwright raises uniQure price target to $80 as Wall Street turns more bullish
news.summary: H.C. Wainwright lifted its price target to $80 from $50, joining Mizuho (to $68 from $35) and other brokers in raising estimates after the FDA's reversal on the AMT-130 regulatory pathway. Analysts cited higher probability-of-success assumptions for US approval of the Huntington's gene therapy.

news.headline: uniQure closes upsized $259 million public offering of ordinary shares
news.summary: uniQure closed an underwritten offering of 5,686,813 ordinary shares at $45.50 each, including full exercise of the underwriters' option, for roughly $259 million in gross proceeds. The company said funds will support AMT-130 commercialization readiness, the required confirmatory trial, and related launch activities.

news.headline: uniQure reports encouraging early AMT-260 data in refractory epilepsy
news.summary: In the first, low-dose cohort of six patients in the Phase I/IIa trial of gene therapy AMT-260 for refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy, three of six patients had 79%-100% reductions in disabling seizures at months four to six, with no related serious adverse events. Enrollment in a higher-dose cohort is ongoing, with updated data expected in H1 2027.

news.headline: Barclays and Cantor Fitzgerald upgrade uniQure to Overweight after FDA meeting
news.summary: Barclays upgraded uniQure to Overweight from Equalweight and raised its price target to $65 from $25, calling the FDA's acceptance of 3-year data for the BLA a 'major positive.' The firm moved up its assumed Huntington's disease launch timeline to H1 2027 and raised its probability-of-success estimate to 65% from 35%.

news.headline: uniQure announces plan for BLA submission for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease after FDA reversal
news.summary: The FDA told uniQure that 3-year Phase I/II data (showing a 75% slowing of disease progression on cUHDRS at the high dose vs. external controls, p=.003) can serve as the primary basis for a BLA seeking accelerated approval, reversing its prior stance; uniQure plans to file in Q3 2026. Shares jumped about 78% on the news.

catalysts.label: US BLA submission for AMT-130 (Huntington's disease)
catalysts.detail: uniQure plans to submit a Biologics License Application seeking accelerated approval based on 3-year Phase I/II data, after the FDA agreed this could serve as the primary basis.

catalysts.label: UK MHRA Marketing Authorization Application (MAA) filing
catalysts.detail: Following a successful pre-submission meeting with the MHRA, uniQure plans to file for UK approval of AMT-130 using the same 3-year dataset.

catalysts.label: 4-year AMT-130 follow-up data
catalysts.detail: Additional long-term follow-up data from the Phase I/II trial is expected; management has said it could be important supporting evidence even though it is not the primary BLA basis.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: uniQure has not yet confirmed an exact date; based on historical timing, second-quarter financial results and a pipeline/regulatory update are expected.

catalysts.label: AMT-260 higher-dose cohort completion and updated epilepsy data
catalysts.detail: Enrollment in the second, higher-dose cohort of the AMT-260 Phase I/IIa trial in refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy is expected to complete around mid-2026, with updated results planned for H1 2027.

catalysts.label: Potential US accelerated approval and commercial launch of AMT-130
catalysts.detail: If the BLA receives priority review, uniQure has flagged a potential US market launch before the end of 2026, though this depends on FDA review timing and outcome.

pulse: uniQure shares have more than doubled since mid-June after the FDA reversed course and agreed that 3-year Phase I/II data can support an accelerated-approval BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, with US and UK filings both targeted for Q3 2026. The company raised about $259 million in a June equity offering to fund launch readiness and the required confirmatory trial, while early AMT-260 gene therapy data in epilepsy also showed encouraging seizure reductions. Sell-side sentiment has turned sharply more bullish with a wave of upgrades and price-target hikes, though the FDA has shifted its position on this program multiple times before and the confirmatory trial design is still being finalized.
